2001-12-01.title: World Briefing | Asia: Japan: Embezzlement At Foreign Ministry
2001-12-01.abstract: Japan's foreign ministry will punish 328 employees for embezzlement after six-month investigation into misuse of nearly $2 million in official funds over last 5 years (S)

2001-12-01.title: Alliance Says It Found School Run By a Titan of Terrorism
2001-12-01.abstract: Northern Alliance discovers small compound in Kunduz used as headquarters by Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan, Central Asia's largest terrorist group, and its leader Juma Namangani, who is thought to have been killed nearby; Namangani's home within compound served as academy for preparing soldiers and terrorists to export international Islamic jihad and emphasized incursions into Uzbekistan, Tajikistan and Kyrgyzstan; academy was abandoned as Northern Alliance forces swept through Kunduz but documents and other materials salvaged confirm Namangani's ambition of fomenting religious war; photo of document showing how to make suitcase bomb (M)

2001-12-01.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: Ruling On School Lunches
2001-12-01.abstract: India's Supreme Court orders all states to provide cooked lunches in all government schools by January 2002 (S)

2001-12-01.title: Bush Administration Says It's Too Early to Deploy International Peacekeepers
2001-12-01.abstract: Bush administration says it is too soon to send international peacekeepers to Afghanistan, calling conditions too uncertain and dangerous; several American allies, including Britain, France, Canada and Turkey, are prepared to send troops for keeping peace and to ensure safety of food convoys; map (M)

2001-12-01.title: East Africans Rekindle Regional Grouping
2001-12-01.abstract: Daniel Arap Moi, Yoweri K Museveni and Benjamin W Mkapa, presidents of Kenya, Uganda, and Tanzania, re-establish East African Community, nearly quarter-century after economic and political union was disbanded because of regional strife; inaugurate joint legislative assembly to handle regional policy matters and court to settle cross-border disputes; initially intend to reduce trade barriers and slash bureaucracies currently interfering with regional growth (M)

2001-12-02.title: In Spain, Thousands Protest New Plans for Universities
2001-12-02.abstract: Tens of thousands of students are joined by university staff and faculty members in demonstration to protest plan to overhaul Spain's universities; Education Min Pilar del Castillo says aim is to improve quality of teaching by instituting new selection procedures for tenured professors and creating national evaluation agency that will rank colleges and improve administration of universities; critis contend overhaul is start of subtle process of privatization, that it handicap public institutions and favors private colleges, which will lead to two-tier system; most of all, they say plan is flawed because government has not proposed any new resources to put new system in place; photo (M)

2001-12-02.title: Australia Migrants, Many Children, Land at Troubled Camp
2001-12-02.abstract: Immigration detention center in Woomera, Australia, holds more than 1,000 people, many of them women and children and many of whom have been in detention for years; 20-acre complex is biggest and most troubled of six centers in Australia that together house more than 2,500 illegal immigrants; detentions have not caused widespread alarm in Australia, but lawyers acting on behalf of detainees are beginning to complain more loudly about their treatment; advocates for immigrants also criticize what they say is increasing evidence of detriorating conditions at Woomera; although Woomera has gloomiest record, assaults by guards and suicide attempts are evident at all centers; drawing by child detainee (M)

2001-12-02.title: Anger Erupts At Mosque, Site of Raid And Arrests
2001-12-02.abstract: Anger Erupts At Mosque, Site of Raid And Arrests**Members of Islamic Cultural Center and mosque in Milan, Italy, protest raid and arrests at center, saying investigations there are trumped up and racist; since Sept 11, officials in Italy and US have been describing place as Europe's logistical center for Al Qaeda terrorist network; center has played recurring role in various terrorist investigations; police say they obtained wiretap evidence of contacts between leader in Milan and adviser to those responsible for 1993 bombing of World Trade Center; key witness in trial of four Al Qaeda members in 1998 bombings of American embassies in Kenya and Tanzania testified that he had visited center, too; terrorist suspect who worked at center was arrested two weeks ago at Milan train station; investigators raided center on Nov 29, taking second man employed there into custody; police say both of arrested men are Al Qaeda recruiters (M)

2001-12-02.title: Taliban Arab, Like Many, Longs for Home but Faces a Doubtful Fate
2001-12-02.abstract: Fate is uncertain for hundreds of foreign soldiers who fought with Taliban and who are now imprisoned by Northern Alliance; they are foreign soldiers in civil war; their rights are uncertain, and no one--neither Northern Alliance, which took them, nor foreign countries, which spawned them--seems to want them anymore; many of men say they heeded call of aged mullahs who told them to wage holy war, and men set off with little sense of where they were going or what their war was about; many say they tried to leave very day they landed; some prisoners of Northern Alliance in Mazar-i-Sharif, including Fahad Nasir, Saudi Taliban fighter, Pakistani Taliban members Muhammed Jamalhuddin and Amzah Zia Shah, and Abdul Satar Nafisi, Pakistani who says he was journalist captured along with Pakistani soldiers, comment; photos (M)

2001-12-02.title: After Years Away From Campus, a Joyful Return
2001-12-02.abstract: Afghanistani women return to Kabul University for first time since October 1996 when Taliban took control and barred them from classes; Farida Afzali comments; photo (M)

2001-12-03.title: Swiss Voters Reject Effort to Abolish Their Army
Arafat Not Present at Gaza Headquarters
2001-12-03.abstract: Israeli missiles landed near the headquarters of the Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat in Gaza today in an apparent response to three suicide bombings in Israel last weekend.

2001-12-03.title: Argentina Limits Withdrawals as Banks Near Collapse
C.I.A. Chief Urges Pakistani to Crack Down on Militants
2001-12-03.abstract: ISLAMABAD, Pakistan, Dec. 3 - George J. Tenet, the director of central intelligence, urged the president of Pakistan in a weekend meeting to go further in cracking down on militant clerics and other extremists, government officials said today.

2001-12-03.title: Afghan Factions Work On a Draft Agreement
2001-12-03.abstract: Delegates from four Afghan factions are in Bonn, Germany, to negotiate over draft of agreement for post-Taliban government drawn up by United Nations; it calls for 28-member executive administration, international security force for capital, and symbolic role for former king, Mohammad Zahir Shah; delegates and diplomats say that if parties agree on draft, four factions will propose 15 names each, complete with biographies and suggested portfolios, to UN special envoy for Afghanistan, Lakhdar Brahimi, who is chairing conference (M)

2001-12-04.title: Spain Pursues Terrorists Among Its Muslim Immigrants
2001-12-04.abstract: Many Muslims in Spain, where immigrant Arab population has swelled to estimated 500,000, are feeling burned in aftermath of September 11 terrorist attacks against US by dawning realization that their community has become busy logistical rear guard, apparently humming with Islamic terrorists; while overwhelming majority of newly arrived immigrants are law-abiding North African laborers, terrorist fringe appears to pose threat that some authorities acknowledge was underestimated before attacks; Mohamed Atta, suspected coordinator of suicide hijackers, visited Spain twice in month before September 11; police have since carried out three roundups of men accused of belonging to Al Qaeda, terrorist network of Osama bin Laden, and are monitoring about 200 Arab immigrants suspected of working with 20 Islamic terrorist cells; mistaken arrest of Ahmad Mardini, Madrid shopkeeper, in antiterrorist dragnet discused; photos (M)

2001-12-04.title: Trial Begins In Death of 21 During Trip In Switzerland
2001-12-04.abstract: Eight former organizers and guides of Swiss adventure company, Adventure World; go on trial on charges connected with deaths of 21 young people who drowned during Alpine canyoning expedition two years ago; guides had decided to continue trip even as thunderstorm was breaking over Saxet Valley near resort town of Interlaken, where trial is being held; young tourists, most of them from Australia, were swept to their deaths when mountain stream they were navigating turned into raging torrent; case reviewed (M)

2001-12-04.title: Taliban Resists In Some Pockets North of Kabul
2001-12-04.abstract: Pentagon reports several pockets of Taliban resistance in northern Afghanistan as Americans marshal forces in south; cites enclave near Mazar-i-Sharif, where up to 2,500 hard-core Taliban and foreign fighters refuse to surrender; approximately 1,200 marines with attack helicopters and armored vehicles are poised to strike from base southwest of Kandahar as opposition Northern Alliance forces close in on last major Taliban stronghold in fierce fighting; Pentagon more than doubles number of Army troops securing Bagram air base outside capital of Kabul to as many as 250; American warplanes continue bombardment of cave-riddled mountain complexes near Jalalabad, where Taliban and Al Qaeda fighters are believed hiding; Pentagon confirms American Special Operations forces are working with opposition groups in Jalalabad area, conducting reconnaissance and calling in air strikes; says they will not go into caves and tunnels to root out Taliban or Al Qaeda forces; map (M)

2001-12-04.title: Growing Marine Force Turns Desolate Airstrip Into a High-Tech Base
2001-12-04.abstract: US marines transform desolate airstrip in southern Afghanistan, seized eight days ago, into heavily-fortified, highly technological base of operations to support final assault on Taliban's last stronghold in Kandahar; photo (M)

2001-12-04.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Egypt: Militants Said To Plot Killings
2001-12-04.abstract: Officer testifying in military trial of 94 suspected Muslim militants in Egypt say they planned to assassinate number of Egyptians and Americans, including Pres Hosni Mubarak; human rights orgns criticize trial because there is no appeal and because charges of violent activity were added after Sept 11, giving appearance that government is using climate against terrorism to settle old scores (S)

2001-12-05.title: World Briefing
2001-12-05.abstract: ASIA JAPAN: AN EQUAL-OPPORTUNITY THRONE The governing Liberal Democratic Party began deliberations on whether the law governing imperial succession should be changed to allow women to ascend the throne. The deliberations began three days after the birth of a baby princess, the first child of Crown Prince Naruhito and Princess Masako, with public support growing to change a 1948 law that allows only male succession. There have been no male births in the imperial family since 1965. Prime Minister Junichiro Koizumi has said he will support a change in the law but has urged careful consultations beforehand.    Howard W. French (NYT).

2001-12-05.title: U.S. Questions if Arafat Can Lead the Palestinians
2001-12-05.abstract: Bush administration is questioning whether Yasir Arafat can lead Palestinians; drops its public calls for Israeli restraint in wake of deadly suicide bombings and shootings by Palestinians; it is unclear whether policy shift is tactical move to pressure Arafat to dismantle terror groups or strategic change; administration denies United States has given Prime Min Ariel Sharon approval to eliminate Arafat as Palestinian leader; denies Israeli airstrikes on Palestinian Authority offices were aimed at Arafat; State Sec Colin L Powell holds terrorist attacks were also assault on Arafat's authority; calls Israeli reprisals justified (M)

2001-12-05.title: World Briefing | Americas: Peru: Maoist Group Suspected In Bombing
2001-12-05.abstract: Terrorists believed to belong to Maoist Shining Path bomb electricity tower east of Lima, Peru (S)

2001-12-05.title: Talks in Bonn End With Deal On Leadership For Afghans
2001-12-05.abstract: Afghan delegates meeting in Bonn, Germany, reach agreement on new post-Taliban government for Afghanistan that will assume power in Kabul on December 22; interim administration will be led by Hamid Karzai, Pashtun leader currently fighting Taliban in home territory of Kandahar; one of five vice chairmen will be Dr Sima Samar, who will also be in charge of women's affairs; Dr Suhaila Seddiqi will be in charge of public health; other appointees not identified; new administration will be made up of chairman, five deputies and 23 other ministers; will take over country's seat at UN; current administration in Kabul, led by former president Burhanuddin Rabbani, will be supplanted; photos (M)

2001-12-05.title: Arms, Books And Noose Were Fixtures At Terror Lair
2001-12-05.abstract: Article describes former Al Qaeda terrorist camp in Rishkhor, Afghanistan, that was destroyed by American warplanes; photo (M)

2001-12-06.title: New Leader Urges Foes To Surrender
2001-12-06.abstract: Hamid Karzai, man appointed to lead Afghanistan's new interim government, says he has begun talks with senior Taliban officials over surrender of Kandahar, last major stronghold of Taliban and Arab fighters loyal to Osama bin Laden; declines to identify Taliban officials or describe nature of attacks; Afghan and Pakistani officials do not believe Karzai is negotiating directly with Mullah Muhammad Omar, Taliban's paramount leader, but through emissary who is member of Karzai's Populzai clan; Karzai's appointment could quicken pace of talks with Taliban holdouts, who are mostly Pashtuns and are cornered in Kandahar, where Karzai's well-respected family lives (M)

2001-12-06.title: In Texas, Donors to Muslim Charity Seethe at Raids by Government
2001-12-06.abstract: About 300 supporters of Holy Land Foundation for Relief and Development gather in Richardson, Texas, for religious vigil; local imam, Dr Nabil Al-Sadoon, leads them in series of noisy chants and protests; protesters had gathered to express their anger at raids conducted earlier this week by federal authorities on four of foundation's offices around country, including its headquarters in Richardson; Pres Bush has said that assets of orgn are being seized because foundation, which collected estimated $13 million last year, is providing money to militant Palestinian group Hamas (M)

2001-12-06.title: Residents Flee Slum in Nairobi After 12 Are Killed in Clashes
Clan Leader Turned Statesman: Hamid Karzai
2001-12-06.abstract: Man in the News biographical sketch of Hamid Karzai, who has been named to lead interim government in Afghanistan; photo (M)

2001-12-06.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Social Democrats Seek Coalition
2001-12-06.abstract: Berlin Social Democrats begin talks to form coalition with former Communist Party of Democratic Socialism, after negotiations with Greens and Free Democrats fail (S)

2001-12-07.title: Surrender Deal May Hinge on Amnesty for Mullah Omar
2001-12-07.abstract: The Taliban agreed on Thursday to surrender the city of Kandahar, but negotiators had yet to resolve the fate of Mullah Muhammad Omar, the Taliban leader.

2001-12-07.title: Renowned New Zealand Yachtsman Killed in Amazon
2001-12-07.abstract: Sir Peter Blake, famous New Zealand yachtsman, is shot and killed by masked robbers on his boat anchored in harbor near mouth of Amazon River in Macapa, Brazil; photos; map; police say he resisted holdup by gang known as Water Rats; two crew members are injured; Blake, 53, led New Zealand crew that won America's Cup in 1995 and 2000; vessel Seamaster was on research expedition (M)

2001-12-07.title: OFFER IS MADE TO SURRENDER TALIBAN'S LAST STRONGHOLD; DEAL MAY HINGE ON AMNESTY
2001-12-07.abstract: Afghan Taliban forces surrounded in Kandahar agree to surrender their last stronghold, but negotiators have yet to resolve crucial issues of fate of leader Muhammad Omar and thousands of Arab fighters loyal to Osama bin Laden; yet uncaptured is bin Laden, who Gen Muhammad Fahim says has retreated to caves of Tora Bora region close to Pakistan frontier; surrender terms call for Omar to cede power to local tribal leader and have soldiers lay down weapons; new Afghan leader Hamid Karzai says Omar and top aides must face trial for crimes, noting that Omar has not even made statement of regret; former Taliban envoy Abdul Salam Zaeef comments in Islamabad; photo; map; intense negotiations and role of various factions detailed (M)

2001-12-07.title: Boycott Threat Is Early Test for Afghan Leadership Pact
2001-12-07.abstract: One day after plans for interim Afghan government are announced, Gen Abdul Rashid Dostum, whose forces dominate much of northern Afghanistan, vows to boycott new administration and says he will not go to Kabul until 'proper government' is in place; his representatives to negotiations in Germany came away with ministries of agriculture, mining and industry in interim government, but he had hoped for foreign ministry as reward for role his forces played in toppling Taliban (M)

2001-12-08.title: Pakistan Ended Aid to Taliban Only Hesitantly
2001-12-08.abstract: Pakistan's intelligence agency provided safe passage for weapons and ammunition to arm Taliban month after Pakistan government agreed to end such support; twice in Oct, Pakistani border guards in Khyber Pass waved on convoys of trucks headed into Afghanistan said to be stocked with rifles, ammunition and rocket-propelled grenade launchers for Taliban fighters; Pakistani military, as promised, has withdrawn advisers from Afghanistan, move Western intelligence officials see as crucial factor in surprisingly swift collapse of Taliban forces when confronted by Northern Alliance; but Pakistan's intelligence agency remains what many describe as state within a state, with independent, and worrying, political tendencies; photos (M)

2001-12-08.title: U.S. Is Voicing Confidence In a Kabul Administration
2001-12-08.abstract: United States special representative for Afghanistan James F Dobbins expresses optimism that interim Afghan government will succeed; cites American involvement, support of Afghanistan's neighbors and large amount of promised foreign aid as indicators of success (M)

2001-12-08.title: Uphill Battle to Take Caves And Maybe bin Laden, Too
2001-12-08.abstract: Anti-Taliban Afghan forces appear stalled in their drive to capture complex of mountain caves near Tora Bora held by Osama bin Laden loyalists; fluid battlefield is described as pockets of confict sprawled across river valley rather than static front; picture of fighting is pieced together by puffs of smoke indicating mortar rounds that are being used by Al Qaeda fighters to probe enemy positions, along with cryptic remarks of local Anti-Taliban commanders; photo (M)

2001-12-08.title: European Critics of U.S. Find That the War Gives Them Little Ammunition
2001-12-08.abstract: European criticism of United States and war in Afghanistan is quelled because Taliban has been overthrown so quickly and images of women enjoying new freedoms are compelling; concern over civilian casualties remains high and some European supporters reiterate that they will not back attack on Iraq (M)

2001-12-08.title: Conference on Biological Weapons Breaks Down Over Divisions
2001-12-08.abstract: Anthrax attacks in United States focus international attention on germ weapons, but countries meeting in Geneva to strengthen global pact banning those weapons are so divided on how to proceed that they suspend gathering for year rather than allow their effort to collapse; three-week review of Biological Weapons Convention of 1972, signed by 144 countries, is held every five years; cold-war-era biological weapons treaty has no system to catch cheaters (M)

2001-12-09.title: Royal Path: Late Births Lose Stigma In Japan
2001-12-09.abstract: Stigma once attached to having first child in one's 30's is fading in Japan, and this trend should get fresh impetus now that Crown Princess Masako has given birth to her first child just days before her 38th birthday; however, women who want to marry and have children after building careers for themselves still face myriad social obstacles; legislators have shelved bill that for first time would have allowed married women to keep their family names legally (M)

2001-12-09.title: Opening a Gateway, Closing In on bin Laden and Setting the Priorities
2001-12-09.abstract: Overview of day's events in war on terrorism in Afghanistan and United States (M)

2001-12-09.title: Canada Relies On Discipline As Economy Slows Down
2001-12-09.abstract: Budget that Finance Min Paul Martin will present to Canadian Parliament is expected to stress fiscal discipline, loosening purse strings only enough to provide a few billion extra dollars for domestic security and military and to improve infrastructure along US borders; foreign aid may increase modestly, but tax cuts are out of question (M)

2001-12-09.title: UZBEKS TO ALLOW OPENING OF BRIDGE ON AFGHAN BORDER
2001-12-09.abstract: After weeks of diplomatic pressure from the United States, the president of Uzbekistan, Islam Karimov, agreed on Saturday to open a crucial bridge that could help speed urgently needed food and medical supplies from this Central Asian country to war-ravaged Afghanistan.     The announcement to open the Friendship Bridge came in a news conference here with Secretary of State Colin L. Powell, who is visiting partners in the United States-led coalition against terrorism. The decision would free international relief organizations to move millions of tons of food and other aid into northern Afghanistan, where wintry weather has already complicated the plight of millions of Afghan civilians threatened by drought and the war.

2001-12-09.title: Nuclear Experts in Pakistan May Have Links to Al Qaeda
2001-12-09.abstract: US is investigating reports of contacts between Pakistani nuclear weapons scientists and Taliban or terrorist network Al Qaeda; seek interogation of Suleiman Asad and Muhammed Ali Mukhtar, nuclear experts with long experience at two of Pakistan's most secret nuclear installations, but Gen Pervez Musharraf, Pakistan's military president, has reportedly arranged temporary asylum for them Myanmar; Pakistani government is apparently suspicious that US, which is also trying to grow closer to India, is using its security concerns as pretext for prying open Pakistan's nuclear weapons program; American officials say two other Pakistani nuclear scientists, Sultan Bashiruddin Mahmood and Chaudry Abdul Majeed, who have been detained and questioned by Pakistan, did meet with Taliban and Al Qaeda officials in Afghanistan to discuss nuclear issues but are not weapons experts and would have been of little value to terrorists; photo (M)

2001-12-10.title: Annan, Accepting Nobel Prize, Restates U.N.'s Continuing Goals
2001-12-10.abstract: OSLO, Dec. 10 — Kofi Annan, the secretary general of the United Nations, used the occasion of the 2001 Nobel Peace Prize lecture today to make an impassioned case for the continued importance of the United Nations as a promoter of peace and a champion of individual rights in an unstable and unequal world.

2001-12-10.title: Annan's Speech
2001-12-10.abstract: Oslo, Dec. 10Following is the full text of Kofi Annan's speech after he was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize.

2001-12-10.title: A Brash Rival for Venezuela's President
2001-12-10.abstract: Caracas Mayor Alfredo Pena, brash rival to Venezuelan Pres Hugo Chavez, calls Chavez policies 'absurd' and throws support behind nationwide work stoppage planned by businessmen protesting Chavez program for economic reform; onetime Chavez ally delights in provoking volatile Chavez and emerges as leading challenger to president who has rolled over opponents in his three-year remaking of Venezuela; photo (M)

2001-12-10.title: More Violence Jolts Mideast Truce Efforts
2001-12-10.abstract: Deaths of four Palestinian police officers, shot by Israeli troops, followed by another Palestinian suicide bombing further jolts attempts to arrange cease-fire; US envoy talks again with security chiefs but warns he may return to US; Prime Min Ariel Sharon warns of more military action after suicide bombing in Haifa; at least seven people are hurt (S)

2001-12-10.title: In Louder Voices, Iranians Talk of Dialogue With U.S.
2001-12-10.abstract: Iranian officials are openly pushing for resumption of dialogue with US for first time since relations were severed in 1980; issue is discussed with increasing boldness in press and parliament and on street, with even leading conservative, Moshen Rezai, acknowledging two countries' common interests in Afghanistan; photos; Iranians at same time resent being repeatedly listed as terrorist-sponsoring state and there is stunned disbelief that Pakistan, which so clearly backed wrong horse in Afghanistan, now reaps benefit of reversing its stand (M)

2001-12-11.title: Italy's Leader Raises Storm by Opposing European Warrant Plan
2001-12-11.abstract: For the first time there is real consternation in Italy over a government policy that even some supporters of Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi see as an effort to help him out of his continuing legal problems.    Italy is blocking the creation of a European arrest warrant that the other 14 European Union countries see as an important new tool for fighting terrorism -- a way of allowing prosecutors to make arrests in other countries without waiting for lengthy extradition proceedings.

2001-12-11.title: Bush's Speech at The Citadel
2001-12-11.abstract: Following is the full text of President Bush's remarks yesterday at the Citadel, as transcribed by the Federal News Service Inc.

2001-12-11.title: World Briefing | Europe: Northern Ireland: Protest Turns Violent
2001-12-11.abstract: More than 100 demonstrators attack two British Army watchtowers and police barracks in Armagh, Northern Ireland, leaving 21 police officers and 3 British soldiers injured (S)

2001-12-11.title: U.S. Mideast Peace Envoy Makes His Impatience Clear
2001-12-11.abstract: Anthony C Zinni, Bush administration's Middle East envoy, expresses exasperation at what he sees as unwillingness of Israeli and Palestinians to engage in serious talks to end violence between them; foreign ministers of European Union demand that Yasir Arafat dismantle 'terrorist networks' of Islamic Jihad and Hamas and make public appeal in Arabic for end to armed intifada; demand that Israel freeze settlements, east restrictions that have Palestinians in virtual lockdown in West Bank and Gaza and end extrajudicial executions (M)

2001-12-11.title: In Nobel Talk, Annan Sees Each Human Life as the Prize
2001-12-11.abstract: Kofi Annan, secretary general of United Nations and co-winner with UN of 2001 Nobel Peace Prize, uses Nobel lecture in Oslo to make impassioned case for continued importance of UN as promoter of peace and champion of individuals rights in unstable and unequal world; says US must not lose sight of its principles in pursuing war against those responsible for Sept 11 terrorist attacks; says $947,000 that comes with Peace Prize, which is to be shared between him and UN, will be pooled for single project to benefit UN; tight security surrounds ceremonies; photo (M)

2001-12-12.title: World Briefing | Asia/Pacific: Nauru: Taking More Asylum Seekers, And Money
2001-12-12.abstract: Island of Nauru agrees to take in more asylum seekers turned away at sea by Australia, in echange for which Australia will increase its payment to Nauru to $15 million, total amounting to more than half tiny island's gross domestic product; number of people held in detention camp in Nauru is now 1,200 (S)

2001-12-12.title: Across Afghanistan, Taliban Soldiers Keep Up the Fight
2001-12-12.abstract: Large swaths of territory across Afghanistan still seem to be in Taliban's grip, even as US military proclaims Taliban a defeated force and Afghanistan in hands of friends; situation in parts of southern and western Afghanistan described; villages nationwide are seeing Taliban soldiers returning home with guns and grievances intact; in areas dominated by ethnic Pashtuns, who constitute bulk of Taliban, former foot soldiers of Mullah Muhammad Omar roam free; some Taliban commanders, allowed to switch sides and keep guns, are switching back and fresh fighting is result (M)

2001-12-12.title: Pilot Trainee Is First to Be Indicted in U.S. Attacks
2001-12-12.abstract: WASHINGTON—  A federal grand jury has indicted a Moroccan-born French national, suspected of having terrorist links even before the Sept. 11 attacks, of conspiring with the Qaida group to join in the plot, Attorney General John Ashcroft said Tuesday.

2001-12-12.title: Beijing Asks For the Return Of Any Chinese Among Taliban
2001-12-12.abstract: Chinese government says it wants any Chinese among captured Taliban fighters in Afghanistan returned to China to face charges of terrorism; uncertain number of Chinese Muslims, members of Uighur ethnic minority, have trained in Osama bin Laden's camps and fought with Taliban (S)

2001-12-12.title: China Joins W.T.O. Ranks
2001-12-12.abstract: China becomes 143d member of World Trade Organization, starting what is expected to be uneasy era of change for one of world's largest and fastest-growing economies (S)

2001-12-13.title: World Briefing | Asia: Vietnam: New Power To Dismiss Leaders
2001-12-13.abstract: Vietnam's increasingly influential national assembly approves constitutional amendment that gives it power to hold no-confidence votes and to dismiss government leaders, including president and prime minister; power does not extend to Communist Party hierarchy and ruling politburo (S)

2001-12-13.title: Seoul Journal; A Cultural Clash Forces Korea to Beware of Dog
2001-12-13.abstract: South Koreans are embroiled in debate over eating of dog meat; country is bent on modernizing and aware that in many parts of world dogs are pets, not food; those defending eating of dog meat claim dogs Westerners think of as pets and those that Koreans think of as food are virtually two different species; number of restaurants in Seoul offer dishes that include dog meat; this could pose problem next May, when South Korea will be co-host of World Cup soccer competition; FIFA, soccer's governing body, has condemned eating of dog meat; photo; map (M)

2001-12-13.title: Bush's Move On ABM Pact Gives Pause To Europeans
2001-12-13.abstract: Bush administration's decision to abrogate 1972 Antiballistic Missile Treaty with Russia expected to increase European concerns about American unilateralism re-emerging as war in Afghanistan appears to wind down; leaders of 15 European Union nations are preparing joint response (M)

2001-12-13.title: Flashback to Deadly Clash of '68 Shakes Mexico
2001-12-13.abstract: Series of chilling black and white photographs mysteriously emerge to offer first graphic documentation of violent role played by Mexico's special security force, called Olympia Battalion, during 1968 clash between soldiers and university students at rally in Mexico City; official accounts maintain that students opened fire and that fewer than 40 people were killed; historians and human rights activists charge that more than 300 were massacred, hundreds of others injured and 2,000 detained; publication of photos comes at time when Mexicans are intensely engaged in national search for truth about most brutal abuses of power in their recent history; Pres Vicente Fox initially pledged to form truth commission to investigate charges of past abuses; he now says he will appoint special prosecutor, compromise to public calls for truth commission; many students arrested in 1968 later joined guerrilla movements that sought to overthrow government; photo of bloodied student (M)

2001-12-13.title: An Elusive bin Laden, Loss of a Bomber, Twin Tower Science
2001-12-13.abstract: Overview of events in war on terrorism, including shifting deadlines for Al Qaeda forces hidden in Tora Bora caves, contradictory reports from Pakistan about whether Taliban or bin Laden forces might have crossed border, crash of B-1B bomber into Indian Ocean near Diego Garcia, immigration authority's new crackdown on Middle Easterners with invalid visas, and scientific probe of what caused collapse of twin towers (M)

2001-12-14.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Office Space For Sinn Fein
2001-12-14.abstract: British government to let Sinn Fein's four members of British Parliament have office space and use House of Commons facilities even if they do not formally take their seats; party refuses to pledge allegiance to British Crown, requirement for occupying Commons seat (S)9

2001-12-14.title: New Leader Meets Players In First-Day Capital Tour
2001-12-14.abstract: Northern Alliance leaders parade interim president Hamid Karzai around Kabul to receive homage of potential allies and foes from various Afghan factions; photo (M)

2001-12-14.title: BIN LADEN, ON TAPE, BOASTS OF TRADE CENTER ATTACKS; U.S. SAYS IT PROVES HIS GUILT
2001-12-14.abstract: United States releases videotape showing Osama bin Laden laughing and boasting about Sept 11 attacks that killed more than 3,000 people; on tape, bin Laden describes how planes flown in suicide missions into World Trade Center towers did far more damage than he imagined they would; Bush administration officials say amateur videotape, which they believe was filmed on Nov 9 at guest house in Kandahar, Afghanistan, is proof that bin Laden was guilty in Sept 11 attacks; say tape was found in late November in house in Jalalabad, and turned over to CIA; will not say if US paid for tape; on tape, bin Laden, speaking in Arabic, indicates that men who carried out plot knew they were on 'martyrdom operation' but did not know details of mission until last minute; says one who knew details was Mohamed Atta, leader of attack, who he says was from Al Qaeda Egyptian group; hourlong tape, with English subtitles provided by US government, shows smiling and relaxed bin Laden paying call on man referred to as 'sheik,' who flatters bin Laden about success of attacks; senior Saudi official identifies man as Sheik al-Ghamdi and describes him as militant cleric; says several of suicide hijackers were members of his tribe; Saudi government moves quickly to condemn bin Laden and others referred to on tape; photos (L)

2001-12-14.title: Mourners Speak of Piety, Not Revenge
2001-12-14.abstract: the funerals: mourners speak of p7

2001-12-14.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Punishment Sought For Journalist
2001-12-14.abstract: Prosecution seeks sentence of nine years of labor for Grigory Pasko, journalist who disclosed Russian Navy dumped nuclear wastes at sea (S)

2001-12-15.title: Looking for bin Laden: Reading the Signs
2001-12-15.abstract: American commanders are relying on high-tech surveillance equipment, surmise and reports from local Afghan fighters in reaching assessment that Osama bin Laden is probably still in Afghanistan mountains near Tora Bora; cite Al Qaeda's fierce defense of area; Gen Tommy R Franks comments (M)

2001-12-15.title: World Briefing | Asia: South Korea: Aide Quits In Bribery Scandal
Oil Drilling by Turks Has Baghdad's Backing
2001-12-15.abstract: Turkish Petroleum International Co, subsidiary of Turkish state-owned oil company, announces that it has signed deal with Iraqi government of Saddam Hussein to drill for oil; based on statements from Turkish Petroleum, it was previously reported that Turks would drill in Kurdish-held territory in northern Iraq beyond Baghdad's control, but announcement describes deal for drilling in Khurmala field, near Kirkuk, which company says has received United Nations approval (S)

2001-12-15.title: U.N. Resolution on Palestinians Vetoed by U.S.
2001-12-15.abstract: The United States used its veto power on the Security Council today to block an Arab-sponsored resolution reaffirming the "essential role'' of the Palestinian Authority in any Mideast peace negotiations.

2001-12-15.title: U.S. Help Sought to Monitor Aid Groups
2001-12-15.abstract: Senior Bush administration officials say at least 39 nations, including several in Mideast, have asked United States to review their systems for regulating charitable groups to help ensure charities do not finance terrorists; says series of technical teams, including Treasury and Federal Bureau of Investigation officials, will visit these nations early in Jan (M)

2001-12-16.title: U.S. Officials Say Al Qaeda Routed From Afghanistan
2001-12-16.abstract: American officials said today that Al Qaeda had been effectively destroyed in Afghanistan, and Afghan commanders declared victory in Tora Bora.

2001-12-16.title: New Clue Fails to Explain Iraq Role in Sept. 11 Attack
2001-12-16.abstract: Revelation that suspected Sept 11 hijacking mastermind Mohamed Atta met in Prague with Iraqi diplomat last April has emerged as object lesson in limits of intelligence reports, rather than as cornerstone of case against Iraq; Iraqi opposition figures insist diplomat was important spy, but US officials say he was minor fuctionary who happens to have same name; Czech leader Vaclav Havel sees seventy percent chance that meeting took place between Atta and Iraqi agent Ahmed Khalil Ibrahim Samir al-Ani; photo (M)

2001-12-16.title: In Village Where Civilians Died, Anger Cannot Be Buried
2001-12-16.abstract: Tiny remote village of Madoo, Afghanistan, grieves for 55 civilians killed by US bombs December 1; photos (M)

2001-12-16.title: U.S. Believes It Has Recorded Radio Messages From bin Laden
2001-12-16.abstract: US has intercepted radio communications that appear to record Osama bin Laden giving orders to Al Qaeda troops in mountains around Tora Bora, where Afghan and US soldiers are engaged in fierce combat with enemy; report is strongest evidence so far that bin Laden has not fled Afghanistan; hundreds of Al Qaeda fighters, mostly rank-and-file, have been killed, surrendered or been captured (M)

2001-12-16.title: Afghan Poets Revive a Literary Tradition
2001-12-16.abstract: Herat Literary Assn meetings strive to keep alive Afghanistan's rich literary tradition in face of warfare; members gather to read Persian and Afghan poetry and their own writings; photo (M)

2001-12-17.title: India Says Arrests Link Militants in Pakistan to Attack
2001-12-17.abstract: Indian police report arresting four people who admit under 'intensive interrogation' that they conspired in attack on Indian Parliament and identify Jaish-e-Muhammad and Lashkar-e-Taiba, militant groups operating openly in Pakistan, as organizers of plot; Comr Ajai Raj Sharman outlines case that mainly points to Jaish-e-Muhammad as prime instigator, with other group playing supporting role; Pakistan calls for joint impartial inquiry; four arrested are Syed Abdul Rehman Gilani, Muhammad Afzal, Shaukat Hussain and his wife, Afsan Guru; photo (M)

2001-12-17.title: Fast Ends in Kabul, But a Hunger Grows For a New, Better Life
2001-12-17.abstract: Gunfire echoes through Kabul to celebrate end of Ramadan and start of Eid al-Fitr feast, but exuberant fusillades seem also to celebrate Afghans' hopes for peace and change; photos (M)

2001-12-17.title: North Korea Is Muted On ABM Pact
2001-12-17.abstract: North Korea notes international community's concern about Pres Bush's decision to withdraw from 1972 Antiballistic Missile Treaty, but adds no criticism of its own; governing party newspaper holds ties with United States have reached 'point of explosion' because of Bush administration's policies; conflicting signals suggest North Korea might be open to dialogue with US and South Korea (M)

2001-12-17.title: Britain Agrees to Lead Force of Peacekeepers in Afghanistan
2001-12-17.abstract: LONDON, Dec. 17 — Prime Minister Tony Blair said today that Britain would contribute up to 1,500 troops to an international peacekeeping force it has agreed to head up in Afghanistan.

2001-12-17.title: Cuba Receives U.S. Shipment, First Purchase Since Embargo
2001-12-17.abstract: First shipment of American goods to be purchased by Cuba since United States imposed trade embargo in 1963 arrives in Havana harbor; is part of $30 million worth of food purchases made possible by recent law exempting food and medicine from embargo; photo (M)

2001-12-18.title: Rumsfeld Sees More Involvement for Russia in NATO
2001-12-18.abstract: Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld emerges from talks with Russian Defense Min Sergei B Ivanov to say he welcomes greater Russian participation in 19-member NATO alliance; says details of how exactly Russia would be integrated into so-called NATO at 20 remain unresolved; two also agree to convene Russian and American technical experts to discuss timetable for deep reductions in offensive nuclear arms pledged by Pres Bush and Pres Putin, as well as ultimate number of weapons allowed and methods of verification; Bush has proposed limiting American stockpile to between 1,700 and 2,200 warheads; Putin's target is level between 1,500 and 2,200; photo (M)

2001-12-18.title: World Briefing | Americas: Dominican Republic: Arrests In Killing
2001-12-18.abstract: Police in Dominican Republic arrest five men in killing of Sen Dario Gomez which was thought to be political but now appears to have been botched robbery attempt (S)

2001-12-18.title: Manchester Journal; The Spice Girl and the Soccer Idol: What a Team!
2001-12-18.abstract: Victoria Adams, known as Posh Spice, and David Beckham, England's leading soccer player, are iconic husband and wife team of modern-day flash Britain; their union has received imprimatur of pop culture pre-eminence: book by Andrew Morton, biographer to Princess Diana; couple's every move is followed breathlessly in press and recorded on more than 60,000 Web sites devoted to them; photo; map (M)

2001-12-18.title: AL QAEDA FLEEING TOWARD PAKISTAN, U.S. OFFICIALS SAY
2001-12-18.abstract: Hundreds of Al Qaeda fighters flee from caves of Tora Bora into snow-capped mountains of Pakistan border, and Osama bin Laden may be among them; United States intelligence officials acknowledge they have lost track of him; also unknown is whereabouts of Taliban leader, Mullah Muhammad Omar; anti-Taliban Afghan fighters, accompanied by US Special operations forces, scour caves and tunnels, not only for Al Qaeda stragglers but also for documents, computer disks and any other information; allied forces meet sporadic resistance from pockets of Taliban fighters, thought to be remnants of rear guard that was largely destroyed by American bombing or had fled; Rear Adm John D Stufflebeem says US forces would not pursue fleeing Al Qaeda fighters into Pakistan but would ask Pakistani Army to capture them; anti-Taliban soldiers in Tora Bora put 19 Al Qaeda prisoners on display for news media; 9 of 19 are said to be Arabs; photos; map (M)

2001-12-18.title: One Certainty So Far For Captured Fighters: Conditions Are Awful
2001-12-18.abstract: Recent string of Northern Alliance victories in Afghanistan has produced flood of prisoners, from foreign Islamic warriors to local conscripts who were taken from villages and pressed into service by Taliban; photos; Red Cross has been able to visit only fraction of prisoners estimated in thousands; makeshift jails and poor conditions described (M)

2001-12-19.title: Seeking Intelligence, F.B.I. to Question Captured Fighters
2001-12-19.abstract: A team of eight F.B.I. agents arrived in Kandahar on Monday to begin interrogating captured Al Qaeda fighters, and American officials hope the questioning will provide a bonanza in the investigation into the workings of the terrorist network.

2001-12-19.title: Seeking Intelligence Trove, F.B.I. Is to Question Captured Fighters
2001-12-19.abstract: Team of eight FBI agents will interrogate captured Al Qaeda fighters at detention center at Kandahar airport; officials hope for information that will cast light on workings of terrorist network; photo (M)

2001-12-19.title: More Tapes Found In Al Qaeda Lairs
2001-12-19.abstract: Pres Bush, meeting with top Congressional leaders, reportedly says more videotapes have been found in caves in Afghanistan abandoned by Al Qaeda forces (M)

2001-12-19.title: World Briefing | Americas: Argentina: President Offers Smaller Budget
2001-12-19.abstract: Argentine Pres Fernando de la Rua offers $39.5 billion 2002 budget, $9 billion cut from 2001 spending; asks for quick approval to spur International Monetary Fund to release $1.3 billion loan; prospects for approval by opposition-controlled Congress are slight; photo (S)

2001-12-19.title: 13 Killed as War Prisoners Revolt at Afghan-Pakistani Border
2001-12-19.abstract: At least 13 people were killed today when captured fighters grabbed weapons from their Pakistani army guards and tried a daring escape as they were being taken to prison.

2001-12-20.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: No Exceptions On Sacred Cows
2001-12-20.abstract: Government of Uttar Pradesh, India's most populous state, completely bans slaughter of cows, eve those that are sickly or produce no milk; move by government led by Hindu nationalist Bharatiya Janata Party comes with elections just months away; in Hindu custom, cows are considered sacred (S)

2001-12-20.title: At Least 35 Injured in Mazar-i-Sharif Blast
2001-12-20.abstract: An explosion ripped through the packed money-changers bazaar in Mazar-i-Sharif this afternoon, injuring at least 35 people.

2001-12-20.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Egypt: Delay In Democracy Case
2001-12-20.abstract: Saad Eddin Ibrahim, democracy advocate whose conviction this year drew widespread criticism of Egyptian government, will remain in jail for at least another month; country's highest appeals court has postponed until Jan 16 decision on whether to overturn his conviction on charges of defaming Egypt by suggesting that parliamentary elections were marred by vote fraud; photo (S)

2001-12-20.title: MARINES AND ARMY MAY SCOUR CAVES, U.S. GENERAL SAYS
2001-12-20.abstract: Gen Tommy R Franks, commander of American forces in Afghanistan, proposes that US marines or Army troops be deployed to comb wild terrain of Tora Bora to determine fate of Osama bin Laden and other Al Qaeda leaders; his proposal stems from recognition that Afghan allies may not have ability or will to establish control over Tora Bora; American military officials say final decision might come in several days, and first troops could arrive soon after; dispatching troops could entail political complications; unlike in northern Afghanistan, this conservative Pashtun region of country is suspicous of Americans, and senior Northern Alliance commanders routinely refuse to acknowledge help they receive from American Special Forces; Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld makes no reference to Franks's proposal, but indicates he has assurance from Hamid Karzai, Afghan leader, that American military could continue to operate in Afghanistan until 'pockets of resistance' are eliminated; photos; map (M)

2001-12-20.title: Unable to Control Protesters, Argentina's President Resigns
2001-12-20.abstract: President Fernando de la Rúa resigned today, halfway through his four-year term, after finding that he was unable to control a rising wave of protests.

2001-12-21.title: South Korea Reports a Widening Income Advantage Over North
2001-12-21.abstract: South Korea reports that its people are now more than twelve times richer on average than North Koreans; average income in 2000 was $9,629 in South Korea, $757 in North Korea (S)

2001-12-21.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Terror Fears Vs. Free Speech
2001-12-21.abstract: Lower house of Russian parliament overwhelmingly endorses amendments that would bar news media and compute networks from 'popularizing or justifying terrorism and extremism' (S)

2001-12-21.title: India Recalls Envoy to Pakistan Over Attack on Parliament
2001-12-21.abstract: India recalled its high commissioner to Pakistan today to show its deep displeasure with what it called Pakistan's failure to crack down on groups that India has accused of carrying out a suicide attack.

2001-12-21.title: U.N. Picks Britain to Run A Security Force for Kabul
2001-12-21.abstract: United Nations Security Council votes to authorize British-led international force to provide security in and around Kabul, Afghanistan; first British marines arrive at Bagram air base and will assist in inauguration of new government to be led by Hamid Karzai; Britain will provide 1,500 soldiers and 21 other nations offer to provide remainder of force expected to number 5,000; photo (M)

2001-12-21.title: 3 Photographers in Tora Bora Are Detained by Afghan Fighters
2001-12-21.abstract: Three photojounalists--David Guttenfelder of Associated Press and Tyler Hicks and Joao Silva of New York Times--are detained by Afghan tribal fighters at behest of American special forces because they had taken pictures of American troops; memory cards for digital cameras are confiscated (M)

2001-12-22.title: India Recalling Pakistan Envoy Over Delhi Raid
2001-12-22.abstract: India today recalled its ambassador to Pakistan and ended bus and train service between the two countries to protest what it called Pakistan's failure to shut down two Pakistan-based groups India has accused of carrying out a suicide attack on Parliament last week.    India last recalled its ambassador to Pakistan 30 years ago, when India and Pakistan were at war. The two nations started the bus service between New Delhi and Lahore almost three years ago in a more hopeful time, intending it to symbolize a new opening up to each other.

2001-12-22.title: World Briefing | Europe: Yugoslavia: Milosevic Challenges His Arrest
2001-12-22.abstract: Slobodan Milosevic files complaint against Netherlands at European Court of Human Rights, claiming he was illegally arrested, transported and held since June at detention center in The Hague; he awaits trial there on war crimes charges (S)

2001-12-22.title: Once Reviled, Ex-Communists Join Coalition To Rule Berlin
2001-12-22.abstract: Leaders of Germany's center-left Social Democratic Party form governing coalition to run Berlin with Party of Democratic Socialists, successor to East Germany's Communist party; their new role is milestone in Berlin for once-reviled Communists (M)

2001-12-22.title: The Marines' 21st-Century Beachhead Is Far Inland
2001-12-22.abstract: Marines, one of most rugged instruments of American military power, are being used by Gen Tommy Frank for quick reconnaissance and raids in Afghanistan, much as he uses small squads of Special Operations forces; deadly skirmish outside Kandahar Dec 7 recounted; Franks is now asking marines to prepare to root out last pockets of Al Qaeda fighters, some 300-1,000, in ravines and caves of Tora Bora in northeast; generals say marines are better suited than regular army units for fast-moving, far-flung unconventional warfare that Sec Donald Rumsfeld vows to wage against terrorists around world (M)

2001-12-22.title: New Clashes in Gaza; Hamas to Limit Suicide Attacks
2001-12-22.abstract: Six Palestinians are killed and dozens injured in clashes with Yasir Arafat's police in Gaza Strip, even as militant group Hamas says that to preserve Palestinian unity it is halting suicide attacks within borders of pre-1967 Israel; in Jabaliya refugee camp, masked gunmen trade shots with masked policemen in worst Palestinian-on-Palestinian violence since 1994; clashes are provoked by Arafat's effort to satisfy Israeli and international demands that he stop attacks on Israel by dismantling organizations that advocate terrorism;photo (M)

2001-12-23.title: Back to the Old Bathhouse: Free to Laugh Once Again
2001-12-23.abstract: Six hamam, or bathhouses, have reopened in Mazar-i-Sharif in last several weeks and women are jubilant and eager to go; under Taliban rule, women were prohibited from going where they pleased, but with such restrictions lifted, they can congregate at hamam without fear (M)

2001-12-23.title: Blair and Putin Agree to Begin Exchange of Intelligence Data
2001-12-23.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair ends two days of talks with Pres Vladimir V Putin of Russia promising new intelligence exchanges and saying relations between Moscow and West are closest they have been in decades; Blair praises Russia for its cooperation with antiterror coalition since Sept 11 attacks on United States, and says other nations back his efforts to extend trust to Moscow; says NATO partners are embracing idea of new relationship with Russia in way that was unthinkable few years ago (M)

2001-12-23.title: Wartime Forges A United Front For Bush Aides
2001-12-23.abstract: Crisis and war force greater level of unity on Pres Bush's circle of national security advisers, even as significant differences persist in their priorities for long-term struggle against terrorism and conflicts that give rise to it; Secretary of State Colin L Powell, called invisible before Sept 11, is now regarded as consummate pragmatist, whose long experience in Army and as chairman of Joint Chiefs of Staff has brought realism to American diplomacy in time of war; Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld, parodied as Dr Strangelove pre-Sept 11, has emerged as dominant spokesman for war effort; Condolezza Rice, national security adviser once criticized for stoking Bush's go-it-alone instincts, emerges as decisive vote in councils of war; Powell, Rumsfeld and Rice still differ, but they keep news of their differences to minimum as they seek to marshal diversity of views for Bush to consider; new sense of unity will be tested in coming weeks, as tensions rise between India and Pakistan, and between Israel and Palestinians; at issue will be question of how deeply US should intervene in longstanding disputes; photo (M)

2001-12-23.title: 'Pragmatic Palestinian' Draws Road Map to Peace
2001-12-23.abstract: Sari Nusseibeh, Palestinians' political representative in Jerusalem, lacks real power but has important bully pulpit, which he choses to use to promote ideas that he believes offer Palestinians and Israelis way out of their mess; his central idea is that Israelis and Palestinians must each give up some of their most cherished dreams; says for Israel that means sharing sovereignty in Jerusalem, and for Palestinians it means surrender of their claim to 'right of return'; photo (M)

2001-12-23.title: Brazil Moves to Protect Jungle Plants From Foreign Biopiracy
2001-12-23.abstract: Brazilian government moves to impose stricter controls on medicinal plants in Amazon region, fearful of what it regards as 'biopiracy' by foreign pharmaceutical companies, universities and laboratories; Brazil seeks to build and profit from domestic biotechnology industry instead of allowing non-Brazilians to get nost of benefits; government also faces growing pressure from shamans and elders of 230 indigenous peoples of Brazil, who worry that they are losing control of tribal wisdom and who also want share of any revenue; country has nearly one-quarter of world's plant species, many of which grow only there and have yet to be tested by Western science but have been used for thousands of years by indigenous peoples to treat variety of ailments; photo; map (M)

2001-12-24.title: ARGENTINE LEADER DECLARES DEFAULT ON BILLIONS IN DEBT
2001-12-24.abstract: Adolfo Rodriguez Saa, moderate Argentine governor, becomes country's caretaker president and immediately declares default on its $132 billion debt, promising to use money saved to create jobs and fortify social programs; default is largest in history, but is not considered imminent threat to other countries because crisis has been slow to unfold and many creditors had opportunities to limit their risk; bank controls remain in effect, and Rodriguez Saa says he has no intention of ending practice of pegging peso's value to dollar; will expand printing of scrip to pay government workers; election for new president will be held on March 3; photo (M)

2001-12-24.title: Scavenging The Harvest Where Bombs Have Plowed
2001-12-24.abstract: Scavengers scour rubble in Tora Bora, Afghanistan, to collect scrap metal left behind from bombing; 15 pounds of scrap brings 25 cents, but unexploded bombs can bring death; soldiers look for bullets and boys collect wood to burn in stoves at home; photos (M)

2001-12-24.title: Japan Talks Tough After a Naval Battle
2001-12-24.abstract: Japan's prime minister, a nationalist who has raised the profile of the country's military, called for increased territorial vigilance after the Japanese Coast Guard traded heavy fire with a vessel suspected of being a North Korean spy ship. The ship sank just inside an economic zone claimed by China, and its crew of 15 is believed to have drowned.    Article, Page A9.

2001-12-24.title: China Jails 6 For Spreading Sect's Material
2001-12-24.abstract: Six people affiliated with Qinghua University in Beijing are given prison sentences of 3 to 12 years for downloading material from banned Falun Gong spiritual movement from Internet and passing it along (M)

2001-12-24.title: Cairo Journal; Muslim Televangelist Delivers a Winning Message
2001-12-24.abstract: Amr Khalid, 34-year-old Egyptian, achieves international as Muslim televangelist; his formula -- speaking vernacular of Egyptian middle class, reducing complicated verses of Koran to simple stories and saying he knows that nobody is perfect -- seems to satisfy urgent hunger in his Muslim listeners for religious model that is neither dangerously political nor terribly strict; photo (M)

2001-12-25.title: Pakistan Leader in Sharp Rebuke to Indian Threat
2001-12-25.abstract: The leaders of Pakistan and India traded stiff warnings today about the risks of a wider conflict between them as their armies traded sporadic artillery fire in Kashmir.

2001-12-25.title: Pakistan Says an Ex-Envoy and Spokesman for the Taliban Is Seeking Asylum
2001-12-25.abstract: Foreign Ministry in Pakistan confirms that application for political asylum has been received from Mullah Abdul Salam Zaeef, former Taliban ambassador to Pakistan; official of ministry gives no indication of whether Pres Pervez Musharraf of Pakistan will favor application, but Western diplomats say they expect Zaeef to be allowed to remain there (M)

2001-12-25.title: Britain Gives Suspect Ship Off Sussex A Clean Bill
2001-12-25.abstract: Exhaustive search of cargo ship MV Nisha intercepted by British Royal Navy in English Channel on tip that it carried 'terrorist material' ends with police saying nothing dangerous is found; crew members, thought to be Indians and Filipinos, were cooperative with searchers (M)

2001-12-25.title: Next, Pavel From Irkutsk Gives Putin A Mouthful
2001-12-25.abstract: Pres Vladimir v Putin spends two and a half hours on national television, taking questions from ordinary Russians; Putin repeatedly tells questioners that Russia will not transform itself overnight, even as he leavens most of his answers with data showing that economy and living conditions are getting better (M)

2001-12-25.title: Plan to Let Nations Declare Bankruptcy Gains
2001-12-25.abstract: Argentina's debt crisis is giving momentum to International Monetary Fund plan to allow nations to declare bankruptcy and seek legal protection from their creditors, much as people and companies can do (M)

2001-12-26.title: Suspect in Bomb Plot Linked to Muslim Extremists
2001-12-26.abstract: The man who threatened to blow up a passenger plane with explosives hidden in his shoes is a British small-time criminal who converted to Islam in prison, the head of a London mosque he attended confirmed today.

2001-12-26.title: Japan Wants to Salvage Boat It Says Fired on Its Coast Guard
2001-12-26.abstract: Japanese officials say they will seek to salvage boat, presumably North Korean, that sank in East China Sea on Dec 22 after intense firefight with Japan's Coast Guard; recovery could give new insight into North Korea's electronic eavesdropping ability (M)

2001-12-26.title: PAKISTAN LEADER IN SHARP REBUKE TO INDIAN THREAT
2001-12-26.abstract: Leaders of Pakistan and India trade stiff warnings about risks of wider conflict as their armies trade sporadic fire in mountains of Kashmir, and continue intensive buildup along 1,800-mile border; India continues to blame Pakistan for suicide attack on Parliament, Prime Min Atal Behari Vajpayee telling Hindu nationalist youth that war is bring thrust on India; Pres Pervez Musharraf responds with speech asserting Pakistani right to match Indian might, although he bluntly acknowledges internal threat from militant Islamists linked to New Delhi attack; Musharraf speaks of 'nightmare' Pakistan has become, pointing finger at militants and venal politicians; adds conciliatory appeal to India to deal with Pakistan on equal footing (M)

2001-12-26.title: Israeli Soldier Is Shot Dead In a Gunfight Near Jordan
2001-12-26.abstract: Arab gunmen ambush Israeli troops twice near border with Jurdan, killing Israeli soldier and wounding four others before attackers are killed by Israeli troops in helicopter gunships; border has been quiet since peace treaty was signed in 1994, with Jordanian and Israeli security forces cooperating to prevent armed infiltrations and attacks across frontier (M)

2001-12-26.title: Hoping a Euro in the Hand Will Be Worth More
2001-12-26.abstract: Many Europeans hope arrival of euro coins and bills on Jan 1 will reduce popular skepticism about currency, which has fallen shy of some of grander ambitions European leaders envisioned for it at its introduction three years ago; practical advantages, such as not having to change money at most borders, will finally become apparent in real life; photo (M)

2001-12-27.title: Shoe-Bomb Suspect Fell in With Extremists
2001-12-27.abstract: Head of London mosque says airline shoe-bomb suspect Richard Reid, son of Jamaican father and British mother, is small-time criminal who converted to Islam in prison and was lured on by Muslim extremists; Abdul Haqq Bakr says Reid took name Abdel Rahim in 1995; photos; Brixton mosque, frequented by many ex-convicts, is also linked to Zacarias Moussaoui, suspected 20th hijacker now held in US; Al Qaeda prisoners have identified Reid photographs as possibly having been in Afghan training camp; Bakr says Reid was gullible 'tester' being used by terrorists (M)

2001-12-27.title: 500 Asylum-Seekers Try to Dash Through Channel Tunnel
2001-12-27.abstract: More than 500 asylum seekers break through fences and storm into Channel Tunnel, making mad dash for Britain with French police in hot pursuit; tunnel is closed for 10 hours, and all refugees are captured; Eurotunnel and British officials complain that locating Red Cross camps so close to tunnel entrance encourages such incidents; French say Britain's generous policies toward asylum-seekers encourages illegal migration; map; photos (M)

2001-12-27.title: World Briefing | Asia: Japan: Compensation For Leprosy Law
2001-12-27.abstract: Japanese government reaches $7.7 million settlement with families of 72 leprosy patients who were segregated from society under 1953 law and 16 who were mistreated; government passed law last summer awarding $116,000 each to about 2,100 leprosy patients for human rights abuses, including forcible internment and sterilization (S)

2001-12-27.title: Terror Tape Was Delivered by Express
2001-12-27.abstract: Breaking with past practices of using personal couriers, Osama bin Laden's latest videotaped statement was delivered by an express package company.

2001-12-28.title: Official Says He Knew of Refugees' Dash Into Channel Tunnel
2001-12-28.abstract: official says he knew of r

2001-12-28.title: Body Confirms Suspicions About Frenchmen in Al Qaeda
2001-12-28.abstract: French national identity card is found on body of young man found dead of hunger and cold near Tora Bora; discovery of body of man, Herve Djamel Loiseau, confirms what French have suspected, that young French Muslims have been fighting by side of Osama bin Laden in so-called Foreign Legion of Al Qaeda; photos (M)

2001-12-28.title: Missed Signals
2001-12-28.abstract: More on Pages B4-5:  A Radical's Journey  A Terrorist Vortex  Borderless Rebels  Transnational Mix

2001-12-28.title: India and Pakistan Add to War Footing
2001-12-28.abstract: India moves short-range ballistic missiles to Pakistan border as part of major military buildup; Min George Fernandes says large-scale mobilization will be finished in two or three days, leaving India 'ready for any eventuality;' India closes its airspace to Pakistan, takes other steps to punish Pakistan for failing to shut down Jaish-e-Muhammad and Lashkar-e-Taiba, groups it blames for attack on Indian Parliament; Pakistan responds with matching punitive steps; photos (M)

2001-12-28.title: Reports Narrow Down Movements Of Man With the Plastic Explosives
2001-12-28.abstract: Investigators in Europe and United States try to establish movements of Richard C Reid in weeks before he is suspected of having tried to blow up trans-Atlantic airliner with explosives hidden in his shoes; federal law-enforcement officials in Washington say material found in Reid's sneakers appears to contain chemical ingredient in plastic explosives and its use suggests fairly sophisticated knowledge of such materials by Reid or any accomplices; other explosives experts say material is so widely used commercially that it is not difficult to obtain (M)

2001-12-29.title: World Briefing | Africa: Zambia: Slow Count Delays Inauguration
2001-12-29.abstract: Zambian officials begin counting votes but results are trickling in so slowly that presidential inauguration is postponed (S)

2001-12-29.title: Msgr. Mattia Pei Shangde, 83, Unofficial Bishop of Beijing
2001-12-29.abstract: Msgr Mattia Pei Shangde, Catholic priest imprisoned for his loyalty to pope and regarded as unofficial bishop of Beijing, dies at 83 (S)

2001-12-29.title: A Coalition Forms
2001-12-29.abstract: A timeline chronicling the progress of the war thus far.

2001-12-29.title: Pakistan Moves Against Groups Named by India
2001-12-29.abstract: Pakistan Pres Pervez Musharraf, moving to defuse threat of war with India, orders arrest of about 50 leading Islamic militants accused by India of mounting suicide attack on parliament; photo; map; India has dismissed Musharraf's earlier steps as cosmetic; Pres Bush, at news conference in Texas, hopes India will take note of arrests and pull back; targets of Pakistani action are Lashkar-e-Taiba and Jaish-e-Muhammad, groups that have come to dominate Pakistani attacks in Kashmir (M)

2001-12-29.title: Gains and Limits in New Low-Risk War
2001-12-29.abstract: Article on US military campaign in Afghanistan as striking success for new style of warfare, in which American commandos took center stage, playing vital role in organizing Afghan resistance and directing punishing airstrikes; novel strategy toppled Taliban, installed friendly government and ensured that Al Qaeda cannot use Afghanistan as base for terrorism, achieving these ends with small number of American ground troops and little political backlash in Muslim world; drawback is that proxy forces may have allowed top Taliban and Qaeda leaders to escape; US intelligence finds low-level militia may have smuggled Arab fighters out of Tora Bora area for up to $5,000 a head; strategy in most unorthodox war reviewed; chronology of US actions since Sept 11 terrorist attacks; maps (L)

2001-12-30.title: Israelis Bring Help to Arabs In a Village Under Siege
2001-12-30.abstract: More than 250 Israeli peace advocates deliver about two tons of food and clothing to 12,000 Palestinians in Beit Umar, West Bank village that has been closed by Israeli military blockade for more than a year; Israeli government say blockades set up in West Bank and Gaza are needed to protect against attacks on military and settlers, but Palestinians and some outside agencies contend blockades have devastated Palestinian economy (M)

2001-12-30.title: About 'A Nation Challenged'
2001-12-30.abstract: While the Dec. 31, 2001, issue of "A Nation Challenged" was the last one scheduled as a separate section of the newspaper, NYTimes.com will continue to run articles on the events of Sept. 11 and their aftermath in this section.

2001-12-30.title: Slowing the Search, Reading the Tea Leaves, Securing the Skies
2001-12-30.abstract: Overview of events in war in Afghanistan and on terrorism (M)

2001-12-30.title: Hunt for bin Laden Loses Steam As Winter Grips Afghan Caves
2001-12-30.abstract: American-backed efforts to comb caves of Tora Bora for signs of Osama bin Laden seem to be fading as top Bush administration priority; it has made little progress; 50 American Special Operations troops remaining in region are not searching caves themselves, and Pentagon has shelved plans to send several hundred marines and Army troops to Tora Bora; instead, military is offering money and warm clothing to Afghan militia members to continue search; administration's slackening pace seems to reflect fresh assessment of bin Laden's whereabouts--or lack of information about his location; map (M)

2001-12-30.title: India Builds Up Forces as Bush Urges Calm
2001-12-30.abstract: Pres Bush calls on leaders of India and Pakistan to try to avert war, but Prime Min Atal Behari Vajpayee rushes Indian troops to border of Pakistan and says India is prepared for war if diplomacy fails; Bush tells Gen Pervez Musharraf of Pakistan that he must do more to rein in terrorists; photo (M)

2001-12-31.title: Banks in Europe Scramble to Fill Automated Tellers With Euros by Midnight
2001-12-31.abstract: Banks across Europe to embark on frenzied money switch on Dec 31, as they try to load most of their 200,000 automated bank tellers with new euro notes before stroke of midnight; euro coins and notes will become legal tender in 12 European nations immediately after midnight, on Jan 1, and they will replace national currencies by end of Feb; banks admit to last-minute anxieties even though they have long been preparing for changeover (M)

2001-12-31.title: The Legacy of the Taliban Is a Sad and Broken Land
2001-12-31.abstract: Afghanistan left behind by fleeing Taliban is sad and broken land; in five years that Taliban ruled, one million refugees joined million others who already left and refused to come home, and six million Afghans, quarter of country, are unable to find enough food; real legacy of Taliban rule lies deeper; journey around country reveals deep psychological wounds in people still struggling with torment of government regarded by many as oppressive and strange; nearly one in eight families in Kabul is believed to be headed by widows, and most have children; under Taliban, they were not permitted to work; Taliban flourished outside gaze of world's remaining superpower, United States, finally giving succor to terrorists that would invite its own doom; photos (L)

2001-12-31.title: Iranian Religious Leader Enters Dispute
2001-12-31.abstract: Iran's supreme religous leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei enters fray in constitutional crisis between hard-line judiciary and more reformist Parliament; at issue is judiciary's jailing of many reformists in recent years, including member of Parliament (S)

2001-12-31.title: Two Worlds Paired by War: Life or Death, as Luck Will Have It
2001-12-31.abstract: Impressions in Afghanistan and at World Trade Center remains recorded by New York Times reporter C J Chivers after spending time and reporting from both sites; in both places, he finds that it is often chance that determines where someone will be and if person will live or die; photos (M)

2001-12-31.title: Istanbul Journal; Lambs to the Slaughter, But Out of Sight, Please
2001-12-31.abstract: Turkish cabinet adopts new rules limiting public slaughter of animals during Kurban Bayrami, one of most important of Islamic holidays there, time when animals, mostly sheep and cattle, are traditionally sacrificed; slice of Turkish society that is anxious to see Turkey join European Union is concerned that slaughter makes Turkey appear uncivilized abroad; photos; map (M)
